commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4fa57ae4412581e6da85ef4390cd8af7419163e2)
commands.review_summary:
Adds canonical session-file ownership and file-indexed active-run lookup for embedded attempts, reply admission, and stuck-session recovery, with focused regressions and a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +280, Tests +156, Docs +1. Total +437 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/proof level: current main lacks file-scoped active-run ownership, the linked issue has real gateway logs for same-file takeover failures, and the PR body includes Crabbox same-file two-lane proof. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A maintainer should resolve the protected-label PR and either remove or explicitly approve the release-owned changelog edit; no broader automated code repair is needed.

target.title: Fix embedded session file ownership race
target.body:
## Summary
- serialize embedded attempts by canonical session file so sibling session keys cannot open overlapping prompt windows on the same transcript
- add session-file active-run indexing for reply admission, interrupt handling, and stuck-session recovery
- drain queued transcript events before reacquiring the prompt lock and add regressions for owner, symlink, registry, and recovery paths

Fixes #85913.

## Verification
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/runs.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.integration.test.ts` - 6 files, 145 tests passed
- AWS Crabbox `run_1d1a8ff1d155`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_41030e1b0cf9`, type `c7a.8xlarge`: `pnpm check:changed`, focused regression tests, and source-level same-file two-lane proof all passed

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Same-process embedded runs that resolve to the same physical transcript file are serialized by session-file owner, and sibling/fallback-key recovery now observes active same-file runs instead of resetting their lanes.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_41030e1b0cf9`, run `run_1d1a8ff1d155`, Node `v24.16.0`, synced branch patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm check:changed`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/runs.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.integration.test.ts`; source-level proof that starts one released prompt owner, attempts a second same-file owner, confirms the second waits, then verifies sibling-key stuck recovery skips because the same session file has an active embedded run.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox proof emitted `OPENCLAW_85913_FIX_PROOF {"status":"fixed","sameFileOrder":["first-released","second-waiting","first-reacquired","second-owner"],"takeoverErrorObserved":false}`.

Observed result after fix: The second same-file attempt did not enter the prompt window until the first attempt reacquired and released its file owner, and same-file sibling recovery returned `status=skipped`, `reason=active_embedded_run` without resetting queued lane work.

What was not tested: A real Telegram bot roundtrip was not rerun for this PR because the fix is in the shared embedded runner/session ownership path and the AWS proof exercises the reported same-file race and highest-risk sibling recovery path without live channel credentials.
target.author: joshavant

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
